annotations.label: Cauchy–Schwarz inequality (statement)

anchor.snippet: The Cauchy–Schwarz inequality states that for all vectors

annotations.note: `norm_inner_le_norm` states ‖⟪x,y⟫‖ ≤ ‖x‖·‖y‖ for any inner product space, the exact Cauchy–Schwarz inequality.

annotations.label: Induced (canonical) norm

anchor.snippet: Every inner product gives rise to a Euclidean

annotations.note: `norm_eq_sqrt_re_inner` records that the norm induced by an inner product is ‖x‖ = √(re⟪x,x⟫).

annotations.label: Equality characterization

anchor.snippet: Moreover, the two sides are equal if and only if

annotations.note: `norm_inner_eq_norm_iff` proves ‖⟪x,y⟫‖ = ‖x‖·‖y‖ iff one nonzero vector is a scalar multiple of the other.

annotations.label: Sedrakyan's inequality

anchor.snippet: Sedrakyan's inequality , also known as

annotations.note: `Finset.sq_sum_div_le_sum_sq_div` is documented as Sedrakyan's/Titu's/Engel's lemma: (∑fᵢ)²/∑gᵢ ≤ ∑ fᵢ²/gᵢ.

annotations.label: Cauchy–Schwarz in the plane R²

anchor.snippet: then the Cauchy–Schwarz inequality becomes

annotations.note: The R² dot-product case is the two-term instance of the general finite-sum Cauchy–Schwarz `Finset.sum_mul_sq_le_sq_mul_sq`.

annotations.label: Cauchy–Schwarz in Rⁿ

anchor.snippet: with the standard inner product, which is the dot product

annotations.note: `Finset.sum_mul_sq_le_sq_mul_sq` is exactly (∑fᵢgᵢ)² ≤ (∑fᵢ²)(∑gᵢ²), the Cauchy–Schwarz inequality for the dot product on Rⁿ.

annotations.label: Cauchy–Schwarz in Cⁿ

anchor.snippet: is the canonical complex inner product

annotations.note: Cⁿ with its canonical inner product is an RCLike inner product space, so the general `inner_mul_inner_self_le` specializes to this case.

annotations.label: Cauchy–Schwarz in L²

anchor.snippet: For the inner product space of square-integrable complex-valued functions

annotations.note: L² is given an inner product space instance (Mathlib.MeasureTheory.Function.L2Space), so the general `norm_inner_le_norm` yields the L² Cauchy–Schwarz inequality.

annotations.label: Triangle inequality consequence

anchor.snippet: the triangle inequality is a consequence of the Cauchy–Schwarz inequality

annotations.note: The triangle inequality ‖x+y‖ ≤ ‖x‖+‖y‖ is `norm_add_le`, proven generally for normed groups (more general than the inner-product-space case).

annotations.label: Continuity of the inner product

anchor.snippet: the inner product is a continuous function

annotations.note: `continuous_inner` states the inner product map (x,y) ↦ ⟪x,y⟫ is continuous.

annotations.label: Angle between vectors

anchor.snippet: allows one to extend the notion of

annotations.note: `InnerProductGeometry.angle x y = arccos(⟪x,y⟫/(‖x‖‖y‖))` is the angle defined via Cauchy–Schwarz.

annotations.label: Covariance inequality

anchor.snippet: Then the covariance inequality

annotations.note: The covariance bilinear form is proven positive semidefinite, but the explicit Cauchy–Schwarz bound Cov(X,Y)² ≤ Var(X)·Var(Y) is not stated.

annotations.label: Mantel's theorem

anchor.snippet: is used to prove Mantel's theorem

annotations.note: Turán's theorem (which generalizes Mantel's r=2 case) is formalized, but the specific Mantel triangle-free edge-count statement ≤ ⌊n²/4⌋ is not separately given.

annotations.label: Cauchy–Schwarz inequality (full theorem)
annotations.note: The full inner-product-space statement ‖⟪u,v⟫‖ ≤ ‖u‖·‖v‖ is `norm_inner_le_norm`.

annotations.label: Hölder generalization

anchor.snippet: Hölder's inequality generalizes it to

annotations.note: `Real.inner_le_Lp_mul_Lq` is Hölder's inequality ∑fᵢgᵢ ≤ (∑|fᵢ|^p)^(1/p)(∑|gᵢ|^q)^(1/q), generalizing Cauchy–Schwarz.

annotations.label: Cauchy–Schwarz for positive functionals on C*-algebras
annotations.note: The GNS construction defines the pre-inner product ⟪a,b⟫ = f(a*·b) from a positive functional, so Cauchy–Schwarz follows by invoking `inner_mul_inner_self_le`, but no standalone named theorem states it.

annotations.label: Kadison–Schwarz inequality
annotations.note: No declaration for the Kadison–Schwarz inequality f(a)*f(a) ≤ f(a*a) for unital 2-positive maps was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Modified Schwarz inequality for 2-positive maps
annotations.note: No Mathlib declaration formalizing the modified Schwarz inequality for 2-positive maps was found.

annotations.label: Callebaut's inequality
annotations.note: No declaration for Callebaut's inequality was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Discriminant proof of Cauchy–Schwarz in Rⁿ

anchor.snippet: Since the latter polynomial is nonnegative, it has at most one real root, hence its discriminant is less than or equal to zero.

annotations.note: `discrim_le_zero` gives that a nonnegative real quadratic has discriminant ≤ 0, the key fact used in this proof of Cauchy–Schwarz on Rⁿ.

annotations.label: Handshaking lemma

anchor.snippet: And then by applying the handshaking lemma

annotations.note: `SimpleGraph.sum_degrees_eq_twice_card_edges` is the handshaking lemma: ∑ deg(v) = 2·|E|.

annotations.label: Inner product space

anchor.snippet: two vectors in an inner product space

annotations.note: `InnerProductSpace 𝕜 E` is the typeclass for a vector space E over 𝕜 = ℝ or ℂ equipped with a (sesqui-)linear positive-definite inner product.

annotations.label: Cauchy–Schwarz: ⟪x,y⟫·⟪y,x⟫ ≤ ⟪x,x⟫·⟪y,y⟫

anchor.snippet: where [MATH] is the inner product

annotations.note: `inner_mul_inner_self_le` gives the unnormed form ‖⟪x,y⟫‖² ≤ re⟪x,x⟫·re⟪y,y⟫.

annotations.label: Linear dependence

anchor.snippet: are linearly dependent

annotations.note: Linear (in)dependence of two vectors is captured by `LinearIndependent`; equality in Cauchy–Schwarz characterises its negation for a pair.

annotations.label: Variance of a random variable

anchor.snippet: denotes variance

annotations.note: `ProbabilityTheory.variance X μ = ∫ (X - 𝔼X)² ∂μ` is the variance used in the covariance inequality.

annotations.label: Covariance of two random variables

anchor.snippet: denotes covariance

annotations.note: `ProbabilityTheory.covariance X Y μ = 𝔼[(X-𝔼X)(Y-𝔼Y)]` is the covariance appearing on the LHS of the covariance inequality.

annotations.label: Norm of a bounded linear operator on a Banach space

anchor.snippet: the definition of the norm of a linear operator on a Banach space

annotations.note: `ContinuousLinearMap.opNorm` defines ‖T‖ = sup ‖T x‖/‖x‖, of which Cauchy–Schwarz is the Hilbert-space case applied to T = ⟪·, y⟫.
